_spec_note: DEPRECATED — use _contract. _spec retained for backward compatibility with AAMS/1.x tooling.
_doc: Minimal Agent Manifest for workspace and documentation conventions. Drop this file into any repository. Idempotent: safe to read multiple times.

discovery._doc: Optional container layer. If a WORKSPACE/ directory exists at repo root and contains a WORKING/ subfolder, the agent MUST use WORKSPACE/WORKING/ as effective root. This enables multi-project repos and monorepos to isolate agent workspaces without changing any other paths.
discovery.detection: if exists(./WORKSPACE/WORKING/) then effective_root = ./WORKSPACE/WORKING else effective_root = ./WORKING
discovery.affects: All paths in workspace.structure are relative to effective_root. entry_point stays at repo root.

structure._doc: Workspace structure conventions. Folders may be created by agents or users following these paths. Never deletes. Never overwrites existing content.

documentation_model._doc: Four-layer documentation model conventions. Describes the relationship between workpaper, whitepaper, diary, and memory layers.

workpaper.purpose: Session- and task-scoped working documents. Created at session start, closed at session end.
workpaper.naming_doc: DATE=YYYY-MM-DD, TOPIC=3-4 letter UPPERCASE tag (e.g. ARCH, LTM, SPEC), SUBTOPIC=3-4 letter UPPERCASE subtag, description=kebab-case. TOPIC enables RFL pattern-matching.

whitepaper.purpose: Stable architecture and system truth. Written once, updated on structural decisions only.

diary.purpose: Temporal index layer. Pointer-only time log — WHAT was touched WHEN, no content duplication. Hierarchical: daily entries → weekly rollup → monthly summary.
diary.compression: daily lines → weekly rollup → monthly summary (429 entries/year max)

memory.purpose: Long-term context store. Persists cross-session knowledge, changes, decisions.

bootstrap_rules.on_entry_point_missing: create READ-AGENT.md with project scan summary
bootstrap_rules.agents_md: create AGENTS.md if missing — bridges AAMS to Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Windsurf and all AGENTS.md-compatible tools
bootstrap_rules._ltm_mode_doc: 'markdown' = ltm-index.md only, zero dependencies, works everywhere. 'vector' = ChromaDB + Python required. Default: 'markdown'. Switch to 'vector' once Python env is set up.
bootstrap_rules._ltm_store_backend_note: OPTIONAL. .agent.json is zero-dependency by design. The backend below is a recommendation, not a requirement. Remove if you don't want ChromaDB. The markdown audit log (ltm_audit_log) works without any vector store.
bootstrap_rules.ltm_store_setup: pip install chromadb  # only needed for vector store; skip for markdown-only LTM

topic_registry._doc: Official TOPIC tags for workpaper naming. Used for RFL pattern-matching. Extensible — new tags may be added.
Official TOPIC tags for workpaper naming. Used for RFL pattern-matching. Extensible — new tags may be added.

file_safety._doc: Describes how agents may handle file deletions outside the workspace. Git history is the source of truth for file relevance.

outside_workspace._doc: Files outside WORKING/ should be treated with caution before deletion.
outside_workspace.check_git_history: git log --all --diff-filter=D -- <file> examines whether a file has been committed in any branch.
outside_workspace.if_history_found: Files with git history may be relevant. Agents should inform the user before deletion.
outside_workspace.if_no_history: Files without git history may be safe to delete. Agents may proceed with caution.
outside_workspace.manual_rm_rf: Never use rm -rf with manual file lists without git verification.

guard._doc: Describes a contract-enforcement pattern. Each tool implements it differently. AAMS describes WHAT to check, not HOW.

manifest_read._doc: Before any write/edit operation, verify the agent has read the manifest contract.
manifest_read.description: Agents should confirm they've loaded the manifest before making changes.

workpaper_open._doc: Before any write/edit operation, verify an active workpaper exists.
workpaper_open.check: WORKING/WORKPAPER/ contains a file with status: open (or tool-equivalent)
workpaper_open.description: All changes should be documented in a session-bound workpaper.

tools_gated._doc: write/edit/apply_patch/bash should be blocked until manifest_read AND workpaper_open are satisfied.

error_format._doc: Error messages describe the state, not the command. Descriptive, not imperative.
error_format.manifest_not_read: GUARD-VERSTOSS: Lies zuerst .agent.json und READ-AGENT.md, bevor du Änderungen vornimmst.
error_format.no_workpaper: GUARD-VERSTOSS: Kein offenes Workpaper unter WORKING/. Lege zuerst ein Workpaper mit status: open an.

implementation._doc: Tool-specific implementations are local adaptations. See WORKING/TOOLS/ for reference implementations.

skills._doc: Describes the relationship between global skill pools and project-local skill adaptations. Skills are crystallized community knowledge — agents may use them as starting points. AAMS is neutral and standardless: describes WHAT a skill CAN be, not HOW to implement it.

global_pool._doc: A global pool of skills is the source of truth. Not to be modified — only referenced.

local_adaptation._doc: When a skill is adapted for a project, it carries its origin as a header comment (# source: origin/skill-name) and may be tailored to the project context.

issue_suggestion._doc: When a detected tool has no skills in the builder, AAMS may suggest creating a new skill entry.
issue_suggestion.format: Tool not found: AAMS detected '<tool>' but has no skills for it. Suggestion: Create issue — WORKING/TOOLS/skills/<tool>/.

relationship._doc: Global → Local: Source → Refinement. Exploration → direct reach.

tool_detection._doc: AAMS erkennt das aufrufende Agent/Coding-Tool PASSIV zur Laufzeit. Nicht aktiv danach suchen. Jedes Tool kann seine eigene Erkennung implementieren. AAMS beschreibt WAS erkannt werden KANN, nicht dass danach gesucht werden MUSS.
tool_detection._doc_passive: Agents should NOT actively search for tool-specific files. Detection happens at runtime via environment variables or config inference. Active globbing for every possible tool is wasteful and misleading.

examples.lm-studio: LM Studio running on localhost (default port 1234), OpenAI-compatible endpoint
examples.ollama: ollama serve running on localhost (default port 11434)
examples.llama.cpp: llama-server or llama.cpp binary running, GGUF model loaded

tool_detection._doc_local_llm: AAMS voll unterstützt lokale LLMs. Unser Setup: LM Studio + qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b. Manchmal Ollama, manchmal llama.cpp direkt. Du entscheidest welches Tool und welches Modell. AAMS ist neutral.

security._doc: Optional security metadata. Describes the security posture of the agent and its environment. All fields optional.

gitignore_patterns._note: WORKING/MEMORY/ is intentionally NOT ignored — ltm-index.md is the shared collaboration artifact. Only binary/runtime artifacts are excluded.
gitignore_patterns._gitkeep_note: Use glob+negation patterns (WORKING/LOGS/*) instead of folder patterns (WORKING/LOGS/) to keep .gitkeep files trackable. See issue #10.

agent_conventions._doc: Agent behavior conventions and session lifecycle patterns. Describes typical workflows — not prescriptive rules.
on_first_entry: 0. PRE-CHECK: If WORKING/WORKPAPER/ already contains workpapers, STOP — run on_session_start instead.
1a. DISCOVERY: If ./WORKSPACE/WORKING/ exists, use it as effective root for all WORKING/ paths. Otherwise use ./WORKING/ directly.
2. Create all workspace.structure folders if missing
3. Scan repository → write first workpaper with sections: session_goal, repository_inventory (file tree + status), key_findings (from README + docs), open_questions, file_protocol, next_steps
on_session_start: 0. VERSION-CHECK: Compare _contract (priority) with local ./.aams-version.installed_version AND _version_date with ./.aams-version.installed_date. If either differs, execute on_update. If _contract not present, compare _spec (legacy fallback).
0a. FALLBACK: If version discrepancy detected, execute on_update before continuing.

3. RFL — Reflection: Determine TOPIC tag for this session. Stage 1: scan WORKPAPER/closed/ for *-{TOPIC}-*. Stage 2: use LTM results. Stage 3: read last closed workpaper. If prior decision conflicts with current goal, flag in workpaper under '## ⚠ RFL Consistency Flag' with source, resolution.
4. Open or create workpaper for this session (naming: {DATE}-{TOPIC}-{SUBTOPIC}-{description}.md)
on_session_end: 1. Complete workpaper (file protocol, decisions, next steps)
2. Update Whitepapers if session contains architectural decisions (knowledge chain: WP → WH → LTM — never skip)
3. Ingest workpaper into memory (after Whitepapers are current — ensures LTM learns fresh truth, not stale WP)
on_update: Compare `_contract` (priority) or `_spec` (legacy fallback) version with local .aams-version
If discrepancy detected: read CHANGELOG.md section for new version
For Minor-Bump (1.x → 1.y): write READ-CHANGELOG.md with new features
For Major-Bump (x.0 → y.0): inform about potential Breaking Changes, read MIGRATIONS.md
Set .aams-version in target repo to new version

version_detection._doc: Mechanism to detect if .agent.json has been updated via curl. Compare _contract (priority) with stored .aams-version. _spec (legacy fallback) used only if _contract not present. Since 2.3.1: also compare _version_date with .aams-version.installed_date.
version_detection.check: if exists(local_version_file) and (local_version != _contract OR local_date != _version_date): trigger on_update
version_detection.fallback_check: if exists(local_version_file) and _contract not present and local_version != _spec: trigger on_update (legacy)
version_detection.update: write new _contract value to local_version_file after on_update completes
